zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud Le Vavasour於1176年6月24日出生在英國英格蘭越可爾哈茲列武德 煙雨 斯托利特斯時，她的父親Sir Robert Le Vavasour22歲，母親Julian De Ros17歲。她於1192年與Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1226年在她的家鄉享年50歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭史洛普爾廷頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud Le Vavasour于1176年6月24日出生在英国英格兰越可爾哈茲列武德 煙雨 斯托利特斯时，她的父亲Sir Robert Le Vavasour22岁，母亲Julian De Ros17岁。她于1192年与Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1226年在她的家乡享年50岁，埋葬在英国英格兰史洛普爾廷頓。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Vavasour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: status name for a feudal tenant ranking immediately below a baron, Old French vavas(s)our, vavasseur, Latin vassus vassorum ‘vassal of vassals’.

variants.name: Bavister
meanings: English: Titford suggests that this is a late variant of Vavasour . It would be a dissimilated form of Vavister and Vavester, variants of Vavasour that are evidenced in Lincs, also in Cambs, whence the name spread into Beds. Compare Willa Vavister, 1579 in IGI (Scopwick, Lincs); Thomas Vavister, 1615 in IGI (Cranwell, Lincs); Mary Vavester, 1708, Joseph Vavisor, 1738 in IGI (New Sleaford, Lincs).

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud Le Vavasour was born on 24 June 1176, in Hazlewood with Storiths, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Robert Le Vavasour, was 22 and her mother, Julian De Ros, was 17. She married Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster in 1192. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1226, in her hometown, at the age of 50, and was buried in Whittington, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud Le Vavasour est née le 24 juin 1176, à Hazlewood with Storiths, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Robert Le Vavasour, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Julian De Ros, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster en 1192. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1226, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterrée à Whittington, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud Le Vavasour am 24. Juni 1176 in Hazlewood with Storiths, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Robert Le Vavasour 22 und ihre Mutter Julian De Ros 17. Sie hat 1192 Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1226 im Alter von 50 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Whittington, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lei sposato Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster nel 1192. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Maud Le Vavasourが1176年6月24日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアハズルオオッド ウィス ストリススで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Le Vavasourは22歳，母Julian De Rosは17歳であった。1192年にTheobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancasterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1226年に故郷で50歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアウィッティントンにて埋葬された。

ko.Hazlewood With Storiths, Yorkshire, Engl: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 하즐루드 위스 스토리스
ko.lifeSummary: Maud Le Vavasour(이)가 1176년 6월 24일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 하즐루드 위스 스토리스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert Le Vavasour(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Julian De Ros(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1192년 Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1226년 그녀의 고향에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 휘틴그턴에 묻혔다.

pt.Hazlewood With Storiths, Yorkshire, Engl: Hazlewood with Storiths, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.East Mersea, Essex, England: East Mersea, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Le Vavasour nasceu em 24 de junho de 1176, em Hazlewood with Storiths, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Robert Le Vavasour, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Julian De Ros, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster em 1192. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1226, em sua cidade natal, com 50 anos, e foi sepultada em Whittington, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Hazlewood With Storiths, Yorkshire, Engl: хазльвуд вит сторитс, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud Le Vavasour родилась 24 июня 1176 г. в местности хазльвуд вит сторитс, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Robert Le Vavasour было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Julian De Ros, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster в 1192 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1226 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 50 лет и была похоронена в местности уиттингтон, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Hazlewood With Storiths, Yorkshire, Engl: Hazlewood with Storiths, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.East Mersea, Essex, England: East Mersea, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud Le Vavasour nació el 24 de junio de 1176, en Hazlewood with Storiths, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Robert Le Vavasour, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Julian De Ros, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster en 1192. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió en 1226, en su pueblo, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultada en Whittington, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
